message:
feat(policy): green-pressure suppression + canary budget rate limiting

Version bump to 0.2.8 with 3 policy engine refinements:

1. Green-pressure suppression: Guard-triggered fallbacks and calibration
   breach accumulation are skipped when disk pressure is green. Inaccurate
   predictions during green pressure are harmless (no deletions occur) and
   should not drive the engine into FallbackSafe unnecessarily.
   - set_pressure_green(bool) method on PolicyEngine
   - pressure_is_green field gates check_guard_triggers() and
     observe_window() breach counting
   - Guard penalty in evaluate() skipped during green pressure so proven
     build artifacts remain cleanable proactively

2. Canary budget exhaustion no longer triggers FallbackSafe: When
   max_canary_deletes_per_hour is reached, further deletions are simply
   capped (returns Keep) without entering FallbackSafe. This avoids
   blocking ALL actions and requiring a long recovery cycle for what is
   just normal hourly rate limiting.

3. Fallback recovery cooldown: Recovery from FallbackSafe now requires
   both enough consecutive clean windows AND a minimum cooldown period
   (min_fallback_secs) to prevent rapid canary↔fallback thrashing.

observe_window() signature updated to accept pressure_is_green parameter;
all 30+ call sites across unit tests, e2e tests, proof harness, stress
harness, and stress tests updated accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.2.7"
+version = "0.2.8"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
